programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the murder of his old friend Mayor Ridgeway. The motive appears political, but things are not as clear-cut as they seem. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jack gets closer to a conman friend who is trying to straighten out his life, but a fatal car crash complicates matters and an investigation ensues. The case leads to a luxury health spa, where Dr Sloan seizes the opportunity to operate undercover

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble's life is in jeopardy when he learns a secret about a champion Irish setter while working with a champion breeder and trainer

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry is called in to investigate when a young woman is accused of murdering a blackmailer who threatened to expose indiscretions from her past to her husband.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The head of a crime ring is defended by Alex's estranged father. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and the team discover a woman's body in a secret tunnel leading to a bank vault and begin checking into her background. DNA evidence leads them to a man employed at a nearby building site, but subsequent developments implicate a millionaire's wayward daughter - and a bogus art dealer with links to the drug trade. Starring David Caruso and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble, employed in a liquor store, is forced to become involved when a youngster holds up the place

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Greevey investigate an attack on a councillor and suspect gang members could be involved, until a link to a Mob boss causes further complications for the detectives

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers faulty bulletproof vests in an abandoned truck filled with stolen USMC merchandise, and attempts to locate the source of the cargo and any vests that could have been sent to military personnel overseas. Crime drama, guest starring Challen Cates, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tries to eradicate drugs from the street. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: James Tennant reports that a local paper has taken a call from a man claiming to have his daughter Amy and an arrest soon follows. programme.desc: Tom's complaints of rising damp fall on deaf ears when Diana is notified her high-yield, high-risk securities are in receivership. Harvey makes a suggestion that he hopes will address both issues. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill

programme.desc: Herr Flick is imprisoned in General Von Klinkerhoffen's dungeon, leaving Rene to rescue him, and there is a plan afoot to micro-photograph Hitler's invasion plans

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler comes face to face with the woman of his dreams and promptly sweeps her off her feet - on to the back of his tandem, and Barry resolves to throw caution to the wind and show his affection for Glenda. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Jean Alexander, Thora Hird and Stephen Lewis alongside stalwarts Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ricky Hanson faces trial for attempted murder, and his lawyer sets about trying to discredit each member of the squad. However, Jack receives a welcome distraction when an old friend asks him to reinvestigate the death of a wealthy businessman, and he, Brian and Gerry spring into action. Guest starring David Troughton, with Amanda Redman, James Bolam, Alun Armstrong and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo, Nikki and Jack travel to war-torn Afghanistan to identify whether human remains are those of a missing British soldier suspected of being captured by Taliban forces five years earlier. However, they soon discover there is more at stake than identifying a soldier's remains as the crime scene is preventing the completion of a charity-run sanitation plant, putting thousands of lives at risk from contaminated water. William Gaminara, Emilia Fox and David Caves star

The Lyell Centre has a new boss in the shape of forensic pathologist Thomas Chamberlain, but he is met with a frosty reception from Nikki, Jack and Clarissa, who are still coming to terms with the death of Leo. Meanwhile, detectives are demanding results in two unsolved cases involving a wealthy mother and son murdered in their London home and the disfigured body of a young woman. Starring Richard Lintern, Emilia Fox, David Caves, Kaye Wragg and Hugo Becker

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The girls can hardly hide their feelings about the impending release of their wayward husbands - Tracey becomes Chigwell's answer to Jane Fonda in the hope of gaining a svelte-like figure for her beloved Darryl, while Sharon prepares to hand over divorce papers to Chris. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie looks to his dad for support, Cindy looks for a way out of the chip shop, and Phil finds new hope when he is visited by a few friends.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle gets unwelcome advice from Pauline and Geoff. Sharon sneaks out to see Phil bumping into Kathy and Ian sees unsuitable candidates for the chip shop.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis Hardy heroically rescues a man from a burning building, while partner Roger Valentine returns to duty only to suffer a panic attack when he's faced with a crisis. Suzie Sim passes her exam and starts looking for a new post, and Adam Okaro sets his sights on becoming Borough Commander, but is annoyed to find he has tough competition

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Clifford is forced to choose between the women in his life and understanding the consequences of his actions, while at the same time coping with a medical dilemma related to someone from the past. Meanwhile, Elliot struggles to deal with the revelations about James's drug addiction. Medical drama starring Peter Wingfield and Paul Bradley

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Student nurse Roxanne endures a terrible first day as the virus shows no signs of stopping its rampage through the wards, prompting Max to propose shutting down the department - much to Jan's disgust. Dillon faces problems of his own as he develops disturbing symptoms, Simon continues his affair with Claire, and Fin deals with further romantic trauma. Medical drama, starring Robert Gwilym, Daniel Rymer and Michael Praed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Two girls go missing in circumstances that echo the Amy Tennant case and Mickey Webb is horrified to find he knows the fretful parents. Yvonne and Laura find the girls, beaten but alive, and as six-year-old Molly is rushed into intensive care, her older sister Charlotte gives the desperate CID officers information leading to an arrest. Former EastEnders actress Hannah Waterman guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie looks to his dad for support, Cindy looks for a way out of the chip shop, and Phil finds new hope when he is visited by a few friends.

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A family feud leads to tragedy, and the team attend a fire at a foreign Embassy. Starring Glen Murphy and Ben Onwukwe

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Clare unwittingly endangers an older patient's life, and although disaster is narrowly averted, David realises the time has come to step back and take a long, hard look at what's causing her erratic behaviour. Drama, starring Gary Mavers, Saskia Wickham and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul draws closer to DS Helen Diamond, but his social life is disrupted by the discovery that a female acquaintance of close friend Brian Taylor has drowned in mysterious circumstances. Burns on the body and links with an illegal laboratory suggest this is not an open-and-shut case and inspire the doctor's resolve to get at the truth. Nigel Le Vaillant and Michael Elphick star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey receive marital advice from Dorien as they prepare to welcome their husbands home from prison, but it is not long before they find themselves making excuses for her to Marcus. Starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Jane discovers Diana using a slingshot armed with acorns to feed squirrels. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: René and his staff devise a plan whereby Leclerc can enter Gruber's room disguised as a fireman to create a diversion so they can blow up the safe

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Ivy receives an anonymous love letter and spends all her time trying to work out who sent it, little realising Compo, Clegg and Foggy already know. However, she soon rules out everyone except Smiler - and promptly resolves to put him straight once and for all

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest struggles to promote understanding when a group of pagans are suspected of killing one of their own. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on a 1980s music station that resulted in the death of a controversial DJ, and looks into the subsequent suicide of the station's owner. Strickland seeks a permanent replacement for the absent Halford, but Pullman and Standing call his bluff

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki is asked to give evidence at the appeal of convicted murderer David Bennetto, and when her testimony leads to his release, she ends up locking horns with DI Rachel Klein, who put the man away six years earlier. It seems the detective is right when another young man is found dead at the scene of the original crime. Has Nikki helped to free a serial killer? Emilia Fox stars, with appearances from Tobias Menzies, Michael Socha and Aaron McCusker

programme.title: Stori Pêl-Droed Cymru

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Dom Does America

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Leonie tries to rectify the aftermath of a robbery, while it remains to be seen if Ana is invested in being with Evan

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Doctors stars Chris Walker, Matthew Chambers, Sarah Moyle, Ian Midlane and Elisabeth Dermot Walsh take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of south-east England, along the way encountering items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Maisie Smith, Zack Morris and Lorraine Stanley, aka Tiffany Butcher, Keegan Baker and Karen Taylor, who chat about their biggest storylines and share backstage gossip and secrets from the set. Stacey also finds time to put in a shift at the nation's favourite launderette - where Lorraine lets her in on the inner workings of the machines and reveals what it was like to work with June Brown, aka Dot

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Arnott is convinced Gates played a part in Jackie's disappearance, so the team uses Fleming to set a trap, hoping he will reveal his involvement in covering up the missing woman's crimes. Thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim has romance on his mind and has organised an elaborate date with Ire with the help of his housemate Emily, but things do not go entirely to plan. Jeremiah tries to rebuild his relationship with his estranged, while Simi is left holding the Speedi Kazz fort, but she may be about to fall into a very dangerous trap

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Green hosts as the contestants' creative marketing skills are put to the test as they write and produce commercials for fictional drag queen law firms

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Former Labour home secretary Alan Johnson takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist and political commentator Tim Shipman joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their oldest case yet, the 1839 murder by drowning of a female passenger aboard a commercial narrow boat in Staffordshire

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a cottage in Romsley near Worcester, a flat in London's East Finchely, and a mid terrace in Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross invites two teams to seek items of value in Grantham town centre, Lincolnshire, with the help of experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe. The host also finds out about four eras of English furniture

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sully's tutoring of Laila causes tension, and a dinner party turns political

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Anna Williamson, Gavin Ramjaun, Martel Maxwell, Rav Wilding and Paul Rose form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple with a £400,000 budget who want to move to rural Shropshire to improve their young daughter's quality of life and reconnect with family roots. While in the county, Alistair becomes an apprentice under the watchful eye of a master craftsman who handmakes horse harnesses and collars, and then puts them to the test on a horse, ploughing a field the Victorian way

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers look back at their favourite inspirational gardens. Plus, advice on using climbing plants to reduce air pollution and planting a window box

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of Northamptonshire and Bedfordshire. Items up for sale include a ring made of lava from Mount Vesuvius and depleted uranium glassware

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An episode from Christmas 2014 in which the fallout continues for the Carters as more lies are exposed. Ronnie and Charlie hand over the ferry tickets and cash to Nick and he says goodbye to his family. As Charlie gets ready to drive him away, Dot is heartbroken that her son is leaving yet again

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Guy finds Ric lying unconscious in theatre, and decides to seize the opportunity he is presented with. It falls to Louis and Donna to uncover his intentions and stop him before it is too late. Sacha is encouraged by Fletch to set things right with Essie, only to discover she has big plans for the future. Nicky is hurt by Cameron's odd behaviour towards her, and receives an unexpected offer

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Fleming resolves to salvage the anti-corruption investigation and goes to worrying lengths to earn Gates' trust while searching for evidence of his involvement in Jackie's crimes. However, her behaviour catches Morton's attention - and he begins to question her loyalty to the unit. Jed Mercurio's police drama, starring Vicky McClure, Lennie James and Neil Morrissey

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle tries to deal with the strain of lockdown by recruiting some help with organising the pantry. Today's guests include morning host Lorraine Kelly, The Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt star Tituss Burgess, while Brooke Lynn Hytes rings in to spill the beans about being a judge on Drag Race Canada

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Surviving the Virus: My Brother & Me
programme.desc: Documentary following doctors and twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken as they experience the professional and personal implications of the Covid-19 pandemic. In the third week of lockdown in the UK, Chris finds himself returning to work on the wards for the first time in ten years. Chris gets to work treating patients on the infectious disease wards, and he quickly learns just how unpredictable the virus can be. Meanwhile back in March 2020, Xand contracts Covid-19, which leaves him tired and sluggish, and he also develops an irregular heartbeat, which he suspects is part of the aftermath of his infection

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the investigation into the mysterious murder of farmer Frederick Murton in Yorkshire in 1933

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell examine an end-terraced house in Birmingham in a bad state of repair, some land in Rainham in Kent and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent needing a total renovation. The new buyers have ambitious plans, but will any of them deliver their projects on budget and on schedule?

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles heads to Stroud to learn about a little-known inventor who changed the landscape of the world

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia struggles with a request for help, Renee feels betrayed, and Max resents medical advice. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Comedians Ken Cheng, Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Zoe Lyons and Ruth Bratt take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Two youngsters are left in charge when the rest of the group heads off to forage for food. Flower gives birth to another litter, and outcast Tosca tries to rejoin the family

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile railway tour of South-east Asia reaches its southern most point in Indonesia, with stop-offs in Java and Jakarta among the highlights. Michael uncovers Indonesia's colonial past as part of a Dutch empire and its rich history as a port and centre of the spice trade, negotiates the city's infamous traffic, takes a ride on the newly launched MRT metro and dines like a local, on Jakarta's famous street food

programme.title: Westminster in Review
programme.desc: Alicia McCarthy looks back at how Westminster has tackled the Budget, Huawei, Black Lives Matter and domestic abuse and other major stories aside from the pandemic

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Some of the most vulnerable members of the species struggle to survive, including two orphans whose mothers were killed and a young female who is finding it difficult to deal with unfamiliar emotions. The new 'gorilla king' also has trouble keeping those in his care under control. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The quarter-finals get under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, with eight frames scheduled to take place in each match at the top and bottom of the draw

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Two more quarter-finals commence at the Crucible Theatre, as day 11 of the marathon tournament continues, with eight frames scheduled to take place on each table

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of Flower's new pups ventures out with the foragers for the first time sooner than anticipated, but learns a valuable lesson about living in the wild and the environment of the Kalahari

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage from Sheffield, as two quarter-finals continue. The players involved were in action this morning, and return to the tables to play the second of three sessions. Only one of the quarter-finals at this stage last year was won by the higher-seeded player, with eventual champion Judd Trump easing to a 13-6 victory over Stephen Maguire

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss, Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith explore Victoria Wood's take on TV. Clips include banal TV presenters Marjorie and Joan and Sally and Martin, Acorn Antiques and a pastiche of a 1960's episode of Coronation Street. The hosts also look at Victoria's pioneering mockumentaries

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The fifth first-round match sees four students from the University of Reading pit their wits against a team from Birkbeck, University of London in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the consequences of the 2003 invasion of Iraq by the US-led coalition, with America forced to return to confront the brutal legacy when Isis emerges to take over huge swathes of the country. Omar Mohmamed, a university professor from Mosul, recalls how the night armed men invaded his city, proclaiming they were the Islamic State. For the next three years, he lived under one of the most brutal regimes the world has ever known, witnessing daily executions, hand cutting and stonings

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Milton Jones, Zoe Lyons and Geoff Norcott. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day 11, featuring the second sessions of two quarter-finals, with a further eight frames scheduled on each table

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights from this afternoon's quarter-finals, as four players took to the tables at the Crucible Theatre once again to get their matches under way

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: The three artists travel to a home in Birmingham, hoping to assist a buyer born on the weekend of the moon landings, who wants to mark her 50th birthday year with something out of this world

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of Flower's new pups ventures out with the foragers for the first time sooner than anticipated, but learns a valuable lesson about living in the wild and the environment of the Kalahari

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds a variety of herbs that thrive in shade to the herb garden and adds summer annuals to the cottage garden. Nick Bailey has taken a rare opportunity to design his town garden from scratch and gives tips on how to plan and lay out a path, while Carol Klein celebrates the hardy geranium and the abundance of varieties that give masses of colour to the summer garden. Plus, the programme finds a Wolverhampton couple who have created a garden full of mystery and imagination

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold action from the latest fixtures, including Leeds Rhinos v St Helens, Warrington Wolves v Hull Kingston Rovers and Salford Red Devils v Hull FC

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The quarter-finals continue in Sheffield, featuring the second session of one match and the concluding session of another, as the first player to reach the semi-finals is decided

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further quarter-final coverage from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where another match is scheduled to reach a conclusion ahead of tomorrow's semi-finals

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Exiled daughter Tosca searches for a roving male to start a new group with, while Flower leaves Shakespeare in charge when she leads the family in search of food

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The remaining two quarter-finals reach their concluding session, with the players needing to reach 13 frames in order to book their place in the semi-finals, which begin tomorrow. Neil Robertson was regarded by many as the favourite to win at the Crucible last year following a stellar season, but the Thunder from Down Under was denied a second world crown by John Higgins, who went on to reach the final for the third successive year

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the last of his two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson encounters bats emerging from a labyrinth of limestone caves to feed on nectar from giant hibiscus flowers, and tiny frogs, smaller than a fingernail, living in patches of hidden emerald forest. Meanwhile, on the white sand beaches, baby green turtles emerge from their nests to make their way to the Caribbean sea. Colin also sees how the modern world is impacting this island paradise, meeting endangered Cuban Crocodiles as well as parrots under pressure from habitat loss

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part two of two. A look at the rocky relationship between Castro and Bill Clinton in the wake of the end of the Cold War, as the loss of Soviet support proved devastating for Cuba's economy and its people, leading to mass immigration to Florida. The programme also examines how Castro assisted Hugo Chavez' rise to power in Venezuela in exchange for oil, and the transition of power from an ageing Castro to his younger brother. Featuring contributions from key figures in this political era, including Clinton himself

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Eddie Izzard hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of stream-of-consciousness humour before introducing routines by The Last Leg star Josh Widdicombe and South African comic Trevor Noah

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the remaining quarter-final matches, as the line-up for the semi-finals was decided in Sheffield, in the final tournament of the season

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Quarter-final action from Sheffield's Crucible Theatre, where the world's elite have gathered for a tournament played in front of a limited number of spectators live at the venue

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The importance of music in people's lives, through the restoration of a pump organ, an accordion and a bass guitar owned by a member of 1970s disco group Hot Chocolate

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Kirsty Wilson go all exotic with bamboos and aeoniums. In contrast, Sophie McKilligan gives an update from her Aberdeen allotment, while George Anderson discusses the art of getting watering right

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace are joined by Italian chef Aldo Zilli, who challenges the six remaining hopefuls to recreate one of his favourite recipes. After battling with unfamiliar ingredients and techniques, the next task sees them serving up a dish inspired by someone they love or admire - knowing that only three of them will be awarded places in the final

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2020 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their oldest case yet, the 1839 murder by drowning of a female passenger aboard a commercial narrow boat in Staffordshire

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers look back at their favourite inspirational gardens. Plus, advice on using climbing plants to reduce air pollution and planting a window box

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Exiled daughter Tosca searches for a roving male to start a new group with, while Flower leaves Shakespeare in charge when she leads the family in search of food

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hi-tech hotel in the self-governing Chinese gambling capital of Macau. Monica gets special access to a secret set of VIP suites dubbed The Mansion, where it takes more than just money to book an exclusive room. Giles visits a special theatre that sends its audience flying through the air, and helps to arrange the flowers for the Chinese New Year celebrations

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Following the wildlife that inhabits the famous American national park, a lost world of deep forests, vast plains and bubbling geysers. The first episode focuses on a variety of species as they struggle to survive the rigours of a freezing six-month winter, revealing that although the herds of elk and bison are weakened by the cold, one animal gets stronger - the wolf

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The opening semi-final gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, with each player needing to win 17 frames over a potential four sessions to reach the final

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The mother of the colony is worried when one of one of her pups goes missing following a battle with rivals the Lazuli colony, which invaded Whiskers' territory. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage from Sheffield, as the second semi-final begins at the Crucible Theatre, with eight frames scheduled to take place. The unseeded Gary Wilson and 16th seed David Gilbert both reached this stage for the first time last year, with Wilson coming up against eventual champion Judd Trump, and Gilbert being edged out in a thriller by four-time winner John Higgins

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Lucy is hired to spend a few days with Lord and Lady Repton, but they have peculiar tastes, while Margaret fears she may be reaping the reward of her public attack on Lydia Quigley. Period drama, starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Margaret's debts are mounting as Lydia's campaigns against her and Florence's religious tirades outside the brothel take a toll on business. If Margaret and North cannot find a way to entice new clients, the business seems set to flounder. Meanwhile, Lucy is finding her new profession more of a struggle than she ever imagined. Period drama set in London in 1763 following a woman as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner, starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul visits the Isle of Mull and boards a boat to the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of 'puffin therapy' before heading to the Firth of Forth and Bass Rock

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Mark Urban

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the opening sessions of the best-of-33 frame semi finals in Sheffield, featuring a potential eight frames in each match as the players adapt to the one-table arena

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist and a self-taught metal sculptor create artworks for lottery winners who want to celebrate the day their lives changed

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: In early summer, mayfly begin to hatch and fisherman Michael Shortt is fly fishing for trout. Amy Burns from RSPB NI travels to island reserves to install cameras and measure eggs

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene of bright green grassy forest, through which a smooth crystal river flows quietly

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further quarter-final coverage from Sheffield, featuring the second sessions of two matches, with the players needing to win 13 frames to progress to the semi-finals

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez investigates the development of Anglo-Saxon art. Examining the Sutton Hoo treasures the Franks Casket, the Staffordshire Hoard and the Lindisfarne Gospels, she uncovers the secret codes and symbols that reveal the pagan past and Christian future of the Anglo-Saxon people, and discusses how their artistic development was brought to an end by the Norman invasion of 1066

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the stories of sculptors John Flaxman, Francis Chantrey and Alfred Gilbert, who bucked trends in the 18th and 19th centuries. He examines the technical breakthroughs behind their key works - such as carving in marble with a pointer machine and the lost-wax technique - which were produced at a time of increased interest in sculptural projects to celebrate the size of the British Empire

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene of bright green grassy forest, through which a smooth crystal river flows quietly

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: In early summer, mayfly begin to hatch and fisherman Michael Shortt is fly fishing for trout. Amy Burns from RSPB NI travels to island reserves to install cameras and measure eggs

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents autumn on the lough, with Frankie and Eddie Roofe taking part in the annual Erne Pike Classic fishing competition and Joe Kelly juggling running an artisan bakery with spending as much time as possible on his parents' island

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a distant waterfall and a bubbling stream against the backdrop of a tumultuous sky

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day 12 continues in Sheffield, with four players taking to the famous Crucible Theatre arena for the concluding evening of the quarter-finals

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britain's most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, in a programme originally shown on its 100th anniversary in 2016. He learns about chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend the UK against terrorist attacks and rogue nations

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Field Marshal Haig plans a new offensive against the Germans, and Capt Blackadder develops a keen interest in art to avoid the bloodshed. However, his newly discovered talents prove so useful to Military Intelligence that he finds himself stuck in the middle of No Man's Land with only his sidekicks for company. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Captain Blackadder is put on trial for killing and eating General Melchett's carrier pigeon. The only thing standing between him and death by firing squad is the finest lawyer in England - but when Baldrick misdirects his request for legal representation, he ends up with George instead. Rowan Atkinson stars in Ben Elton and Richard Curtis's comedy, with Tony Robinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The drone launch appears to have been successful, albeit at a terrible cost, but just as life in Brizan is beginning to return to a sense of normality, Thomas spots the cloud again. French supernatural drama, starring Gael Raes

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The authorities put Brizan into quarantine and everyone now realises that Ben and Faust have been right all along in making the connection between the cloud and the strange events that have followed its appearance. Then, Ben works out that the solution to the crisis must come from the surfers themselves. In French

programme.title: Bunkers, Brutalism and Bloodymindedness: Concrete Poetry with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades presents a two-part documentary that celebrates 20th-century Brutalist architecture, which typically takes the form of block-like concrete constructions. In the first edition, he traces its origins in the Martial architecture of the Second World War, the Modern Gothic style - widely despised during the Victorian era - and even further back in the time of John Vanbrugh

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents winter on the lough, with migratory birds from colder climates arriving and painter Lorna Smyth putting the finishing touches to her exhibition of landscapes. At Portora's lock gates, commercial fisherman Eugene Brazil and Roy Shaw clean and checking their nets for European silver eels

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a beautiful summer getaway with rugged brown peaks, clear blue waters and a cosy little cabin

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the second semi-final from Sheffield, as the players look to gain an early advantage in the first of four scheduled sessions, and a potential 33 frames

programme.title: Burma's Secret Jungle War with Joe Simpson
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the mountaineer travels to Burma to tell the story of a guerilla campaign fought behind Japanese lines in the Second World War, which his own father was a part of. Guided by his father's old maps and diaries, he goes in search of the airstrip where the special forces soldiers arrived in the country. However, his journey is thrown into upheaval by current political events in Myanmar

programme.title: Burma's Secret Jungle War with Joe Simpson
programme.desc: The mountaineer's trek through the Burmese jungle faces an unexpected diversion when the first free elections in the country since 1990 trigger unrest, and force Joe to divert his journey to Bhamo, a small town north-east of Myanmar. The hills and ridges where his father fought during the Second World War are within sight, but he is not permitted to go there. Once the chaos settles, Joe and his guide Ed Stafford continue their mission, following a river to the settlements and battlefields where Allied special forces clashed with the Japanese military. He meets villagers who still remember the Chindits, and visits the spot where his father's commanding officer was beheaded by an enemy combatant, and the experience provides him with a deeper respect for what Lt Ian Simpson accomplished

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and Maggie Aderin-Pocock revisit the BBC's archive in a programme dedicated to humanity's ongoing fascination with the planet Mars

programme.title: The Mystery of Van Gogh's Ear
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman joins forces with 'art sleuth' Bernadette Murphy in a bid to solve one of the greatest and bloodiest mysteries of the art world - why did Vincent Van Gogh take a blade to his own ear in December, 1888? The pair set out to discover why the Dutch post-impressionist chose to mutilate himself, and also to identify the unknown girl to whom he is reported to have given is severed ear. Their quest takes them from Vincent's former home in the south of France to the Van Gogh Museum in Amsterdam, and along the way, they seek to determine the causes of the painter's debilitating mental turmoil. The answers they are looking for, however, might not lie in Europe. Instead, a library in California may hold crucial evidence that - if verified - could cause an art world sensation

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford travels to the Hague and Amsterdam to find out what inspired artists such as Rembrandt and Ruysch to create anatomical paintings in the 17th century, and learns how dissecting bodies became considered a suitable subject for art in the period

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the story of the two brilliant but controversial Hunter brothers, who transformed both medicine and art in 18th-century Britain. Their belief that students could only learn anatomy by carrying out dissections created an unprecedented demand for dead bodies - as well as a market for the growing trade of body-snatching from graveyards

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are out tobogganing with Duggee when they spot a migrating bird fall from the sky

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Enid the cat is stuck up the tree, and Duggee and the squirrels must think of a way to get her down

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jet Cadets take to the skies in Argentina in an attempt to pass their Academy Gliding Test. As gliders do not have engines, Xuli is teaching two other pilot cadets how to glide using thermals to keep them up in the air

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter, Benjamin and Lily are tempted by a mysterious hoard of radishes, Lily finds herself caught in Mr Tod's trap. Can Peter find a way to rescue Lily before Tod reaches her?

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: With Bitz on drums, Bob on guitar and Bevel on vocals, the Booyahs are ready to perform

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octopod is attacked by a giant squid that drags the ship down an abyssal trench, so Barnacles and Kwazii are forced to swim outside to confront the creature

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has to pass the time in a queue as he waits for his turn to play a cool game.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Somehow, the letter O accidentally pops I's important dot

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: When the power goes off in Justin's House, Justin and Robert must learn to work together to get the lights and TV working again, in order to watch their favourite TV programmes

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Comedy series for pre-school youngsters

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy refuses to have a bath - until his teddy bear gets dirty too

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug escape a very noisy bookshop to find Granny Pug, who is leading a mediation retreat. They are trying to find a peaceful spot - sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't. They return to the bookshop where Dog can make some noise

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The children make heads out of egg shells and cress seeds

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky's bag fills with sparkles from a tap and Home Dome overflows with Tubby Custard until Po turns the Tubby Custard Machine off. A child in Tummy Tales uses a new tap in the bathroom when she brushes her teeth before bedtime

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Miss Grizzlesniff organises a game of indoor hoopla on a rainy day

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo likes plastic ice-cream spoons even more than ice-cream. He keeps them and makes them his friends, but Draff says he cannot keep and make friends with everything

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Moth seems to be the only one on the patchwork blanket. Will she be able to find all her friends?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Bobby and Lenny make a special roast dinner for the family. However, before they can help with the cooking they need to head to the local shop to buy the ingredients

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's teacher comes for a home visit and the Brooklyn-Bells want Waffle to be on his best behaviour, but Waffle cannot stop chewing everything

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Mitten is inconsolable when Yabba and Timmy break her new music box

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The tea-loving bird-like characters see a man take his chair out into the garden, and decide to bring some more things outside. Meanwhile, Peekaboo gets stuck outside the boat

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Time for School

She drives a snow mobile, shows us lots of amazing snow blowers and then drives up a mountain in a snow groomer.

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog tries to make jam, but Naughty Monkey steals his fruit - so the squirrels try to help catch him

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Squirrel Nutkin upsets Old Brown by taking his glasses, it is up to Peter and his friends to put things right

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day heads 160 million years into the past in search of a feather belonging to a tiny prehistoric dinosaur, the epidexipteryx, but he is left stranded in the tree tops after encountering a meat-eating dinosaur

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Purl have started the Amazing Owl Club, with its own rulebook, a members-only clubhouse and - best of all - a special badge with flapping owl wings

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch uses some bad manners in Mongolia, so it's down to the Go Jetters to take the reins. Cartoon following four superheroes as they explore the world

programme.title: Apple Tree House

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Anna Williamson, Gavin Ramjaun, Martel Maxwell, Rav Wilding and Paul Rose form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple with a £400,000 budget who want to move to rural Shropshire to improve their young daughter's quality of life and reconnect with family roots. While in the county, Alistair becomes an apprentice under the watchful eye of a master craftsman who handmakes horse harnesses and collars, and then puts them to the test on a horse, ploughing a field the Victorian way

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers look back at their favourite inspirational gardens. Plus, advice on using climbing plants to reduce air pollution and planting a window box

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of Northamptonshire and Bedfordshire. Items up for sale include a ring made of lava from Mount Vesuvius and depleted uranium glassware

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An episode from Christmas 2014 in which the fallout continues for the Carters as more lies are exposed. Ronnie and Charlie hand over the ferry tickets and cash to Nick and he says goodbye to his family. As Charlie gets ready to drive him away, Dot is heartbroken that her son is leaving yet again

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Guy finds Ric lying unconscious in theatre, and decides to seize the opportunity he is presented with. It falls to Louis and Donna to uncover his intentions and stop him before it is too late. Sacha is encouraged by Fletch to set things right with Essie, only to discover she has big plans for the future. Nicky is hurt by Cameron's odd behaviour towards her, and receives an unexpected offer

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Fleming resolves to salvage the anti-corruption investigation and goes to worrying lengths to earn Gates' trust while searching for evidence of his involvement in Jackie's crimes. However, her behaviour catches Morton's attention - and he begins to question her loyalty to the unit. Jed Mercurio's police drama, starring Vicky McClure, Lennie James and Neil Morrissey

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle tries to deal with the strain of lockdown by recruiting some help with organising the pantry. Today's guests include morning host Lorraine Kelly, The Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt star Tituss Burgess, while Brooke Lynn Hytes rings in to spill the beans about being a judge on Drag Race Canada

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Surviving the Virus: My Brother & Me
programme.desc: Documentary following doctors and twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken as they experience the professional and personal implications of the Covid-19 pandemic. In the third week of lockdown in the UK, Chris finds himself returning to work on the wards for the first time in ten years. Chris gets to work treating patients on the infectious disease wards, and he quickly learns just how unpredictable the virus can be. Meanwhile back in March 2020, Xand contracts Covid-19, which leaves him tired and sluggish, and he also develops an irregular heartbeat, which he suspects is part of the aftermath of his infection

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the investigation into the mysterious murder of farmer Frederick Murton in Yorkshire in 1933

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell examine an end-terraced house in Birmingham in a bad state of repair, some land in Rainham in Kent and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent needing a total renovation. The new buyers have ambitious plans, but will any of them deliver their projects on budget and on schedule?

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles heads to Stroud to learn about a little-known inventor who changed the landscape of the world

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia struggles with a request for help, Renee feels betrayed, and Max resents medical advice. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Comedians Ken Cheng, Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Zoe Lyons and Ruth Bratt take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Father
programme.desc: The emotional story of Jackie Saleh's fight to find her three children after their were kidnapped by their father in 1986 and taken to Yemen. Police discovered that Jackie's Yemeni husband Sadek Saleh had boarded a plane to Saudi Arabia, but the British government were powerless to trace the children. Three decades after their disappearance, her youngest daughter Sofia, who was a toddler when she vanished, is finally returning to Cardiff with her own family, fleeing the conflict in Yemen which saw their house being bombed. The programme follows the mother and daughter as they are reunited for the first time, as they attempt to form a relationship which was taken from them decades ago

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.desc: Dundee United v Hibernian. Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the Premiership encounter at Tannadice. With analysis from Julie Fleeting and Steven O'Donnell

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Surviving the Virus: My Brother & Me
programme.desc: Documentary following doctors and twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken as they experience the professional and personal implications of the Covid-19 pandemic. In the third week of lockdown in the UK, Chris finds himself returning to work on the wards for the first time in ten years. Chris gets to work treating patients on the infectious disease wards, and he quickly learns just how unpredictable the virus can be. Meanwhile back in March 2020, Xand contracts Covid-19, which leaves him tired and sluggish, and he also develops an irregular heartbeat, which he suspects is part of the aftermath of his infection
