programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Ashford in Kent, Egremont in Cumbria and Newport in South Wales

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail

programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Maisie Smith, Zack Morris and Lorraine Stanley, aka Tiffany Butcher, Keegan Baker and Karen Taylor, who chat about their biggest storylines and share backstage gossip and secrets from the set. Stacey also finds time to put in a shift at the nation's favourite launderette - where Lorraine lets her in on the inner workings of the machines and reveals what it was like to work with June Brown, aka Dot

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Arnott is convinced Gates played a part in Jackie's disappearance, so the team uses Fleming to set a trap, hoping he will reveal his involvement in covering up the missing woman's crimes. Thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present more films from the magazine programme that were made in and about Northern Ireland, featuring reports on the bridge to Scotland and Enniskillen bog butter

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim has romance on his mind and has organised an elaborate date with Ire with the help of his housemate Emily, but things do not go entirely to plan. programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Vet Romain Pizzi is at Five Sisters Zoo checking out Billy the coati, who has been suffering from stiffness, and Lewy the ruffed lemur. X-rays reveal good news and bad news

programme.title: Sgeulachd Deacon Brodie
programme.desc: John Morrison explores the life of Edinburgh-born William Brodie, whose criminal dealings and double identity is believed to have inspired Robert Louis Stevenson's character Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. The presenter investigates why the ambiguous man who was hanged for his affairs in 1889 is still regarded with affection in the Scottish capital, with the help of expert opinion and drama reconstruction

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Neen Mackay has a passion for eating well and believes that changing people's diet can have a dramatic effect on their health. The Munro family from Skye tests the theories as its members take on the challenge to alter their diet.

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri returns from prison to Camus alone, which stirs some strong emotions within the community. Cailean is furious she's back, certain that Màiri and Tormod set him up, but it causes nothing but strife for heavily pregnant Sarah Jane

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain Mackinnon presents psalms, hymns and personal faith stories. Maeve MacKinnon sings Mo Chreideamh ‘G Amharc Suas and Rosemary Ward explains the role of faith in her life

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Lon le Linda
programme.desc: Margaret Mary and Aneirin help Linda make blackcurrant rice pudding, and learn about how blackcurrant juice is made

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: Ellie shows Witzy that the clouds can tell many interesting stories

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Tha Bing ‘s a charaidean a cluich geama spòrsail ‘s iad a’ leigeil orra gur e rannsachairean-nàdar a th’ annta ‘s iad a’ lorg rudan le dathan sònraichte. Ge-tà, chan eil Bing a’ cumail sùil ro gheur air na tha e a’ togail…! Bing and his friends are playing a really fun game where they pretend to be nature explorers as they look for things in the garden that match their colour wheel. However, Bing doesn’t pay quite enough attention to the things he is picking up!

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: Òrain mu bhith air a’ chroit bho Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh agus sgioba CBeebies ALBA. A selection of crofting songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters.

programme.title: Na Floogals
programme.desc: The Floogals put themselves in a spin when they stumble upon the Hooman's laundry room

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Donald Smith reads Beatha Dhìomhair Tìgeir by Emilia Dziubak, illustrated by Przemyslaw Wechterowicz and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Lena and Basti are given the opportunity to show that they can be proper little heroes. Short Gaelic film for younger viewers

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Mùmantrobha is left home alone for the first time and is very nervous about it

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: The competitors are very lively, as they take part in hurdling

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Experiments include measuring a person's heartbeat with a drinking straw and making a curly ribbon. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the history of Highland place names in Assynt, and is joined by writer Finlay MacLeod to climb the 613m-high hill Stac Pollaidh. He also meets actor Kenny Macrae on Suilven - the area's equivalent of the Matterhorn

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Examining fitness trends from the past, with a look at the fitness studio and an exercise machine

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: At Tingwall Airport in Shetland, Jim Gray performs the daily runway inspection, but knows that unless the fog clears there will be no flying. Nick McLeod fulfills a childhood dream by building his own Eurofox plane

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee present highlights of day two of the International Sheep Dog Trial in England as handlers strive for one of 15 places to compete in the final Supreme competition

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Motherwell v Livingston. A chance to see the match from Fir Park

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Lewis and Belfast in 2015 and 2016, featuring the likes of Dónal Clancy, Alasdair White, Seán Mac Corraidh and Paul Meehan

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts are in Bradford, where Tim Hogarth has his eye on a silver sculpture of a stag and Michael Hogben's finds include a wooden portrait and a gold pocket watch

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five drag artists from London answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Euen, Peter, Chanelle and Linda pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap 1970s-themed weekender, but Tracey is about to receive some eye-opening news that could spoil her fun. First of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: The Parachute Murder Plot
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce examines the case of Emile Cilliers, who was convicted in 2018 of the attempted murder of his wife Victoria, having tampered with her parachute before a routine skydive. He also deliberately caused a gas leak at the family home. With access to hours of police tapes, thousands of the couple's intimate text messages, unseen home movies and interviews with key people - some of whom are speaking publicly for the first time - Fiona goes beyond the headlines, shedding new light on the crime itself, the investigation and the subsequent prosecution

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress introduces more unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, this time focusing on her journeys to India and Uzbekistan. Joanna shares some previously unseen moments and provides viewers with an insight into the making of the series. In this episode, she travels the length of India, including footage of her encounters with the flower-sellers of Madurai, before taking us back to Uzbekistan, where she reveals some gory tales of the country's history and discovers the secrets of gem-making

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces more clips and profiles from the ITV sport archives, including a look at boxing star Nicola Adams, the story of rugby resilience in the Japanese city of Kamaishi, and golfer Seve Ballesteros playing for the cameras in the company of Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to parathas, showcasing her own recipe for potato and cauliflower parathas served with kuchumber and introducing a report by food blogger Shashank Agarwal on an area of Delhi famous for the flatbreads. Parveen offers tips on different types of flour and prepares samosas with a spicy potato and pea filling

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson presents the show from the Devonshire Dome in Buxton, where finds brought in for auction include the death mask of romantic poet John Keats, an unusual corkscrew, and an art deco diamond watch

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of school friends from Kent answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as John, Deborah, Will and Carol pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al welcomes his first fishing guests, but an uninvited one means that a good review is definitely not guaranteed, while Bert decides to try to rejuvenate trade at the restaurant by joining an online voucher scheme. Morwenna asks Martin for a pay rise, and Louisa has to find a new babysitter to look after James Henry and reluctantly decides to offer Janice a trial. Penhale is excited to receive his new police issue Taser, but Portwenn does not exactly offer him plenty of opportunities to try it out. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants in the studio hoping to sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: A middle-class black woman is reunited with her working class white ex-boyfriend at a party. While waiting for a taxi, she talks over the past with him in a way they never had before, and reflect on the role racism and class have played in their relationship. Starring Adelayo Adedayo and Joe Cole

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress presents a final selection of previously unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, revealing unseen stories and special moments of how these journeys are made. This one follows her adventures in and around Iran, from where she followed the Silk Road through Azerbaijan and Georgia, finally moving on to Venice. The programme also presents behind the scenes footage from Joanna's travels in Haiti and Cuba, including previously unseen footage of her other adventures in the Caribbean

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: The establishment hosts a dinner dance, John Williams launches a new ingredient onto the menu and some Spanish superstars arrive to prepare for Prince Charles's 70th birthday party. The programme also shows how the hotel is turned around overnight for Christmas, with a 25ft Christmas tree beig installed and the kitchen staff preparing up to 500 Michelin-star standard turkey roast dinners a week

programme.title: Unforgettable RWC Moments
programme.desc: A look back at classic action from the Rugby World Cup, featuring examples of outstanding individual performances from the likes of Serge Blanco, Jonah Lomu, and Jonny Wilkinson, plus memorable team triumphs such as South Africa's tournament win on home soil in 1995

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Llandudno, where James Layte seriously undervalues some cutlery, and a Chinese wall plaque sells for a huge profit at auction

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five colleagues from a dental practice in Birmingham calling themselves the Wisdom Teeth answer questions on Christmas films, Italian cities and Take That singles. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Tom, Julie, Marie and Ian pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian travels across South Africa and Zambia helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into the wild. As the trip begins he calls at a penguin sanctuary, visits an elephant nursery - where he helps to bottle-feed and wash a six-month-old calf - and takes some lion cubs for a walk at a rehabilitation centre

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: West Ham player Bobby leads England to World Cup glory at Wembley and he and Tina are reunited amidst the raucous celebrations. Fame and fortune follow as the couple become icons of their time, and when baby Dean arrives Tina thinks she has it all. The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Lionel Richie talks about growing up in Alabama against a background of racial segregation and his rise to fame after a string of hits with the Commodores and as a solo artist. He also opens up to Piers about his two failed marriages and adopting daughter Nicole, who he supported through drugs rehabilitation

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives at the vineyards of Sonoma County, where he visits a winery, and a farm and restaurant run by a couple whose culinary passion leaves James overwhelmed. He also meets a Tibetan artist who is undertaking a breathtaking five-year ceremonial painting

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Llandudno, where James Layte seriously undervalues some cutlery, and a Chinese wall plaque sells for a huge profit at auction

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five colleagues from a dental practice in Birmingham calling themselves the Wisdom Teeth answer questions on Christmas films, Italian cities and Take That singles. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Tom, Julie, Marie and Ian pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian travels across South Africa and Zambia helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into the wild. As the trip begins he calls at a penguin sanctuary, visits an elephant nursery - where he helps to bottle-feed and wash a six-month-old calf - and takes some lion cubs for a walk at a rehabilitation centre

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: West Ham player Bobby leads England to World Cup glory at Wembley and he and Tina are reunited amidst the raucous celebrations. Fame and fortune follow as the couple become icons of their time, and when baby Dean arrives Tina thinks she has it all. The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Lionel Richie talks about growing up in Alabama against a background of racial segregation and his rise to fame after a string of hits with the Commodores and as a solo artist. He also opens up to Piers about his two failed marriages and adopting daughter Nicole, who he supported through drugs rehabilitation

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives at the vineyards of Sonoma County, where he visits a winery, and a farm and restaurant run by a couple whose culinary passion leaves James overwhelmed. He also meets a Tibetan artist who is undertaking a breathtaking five-year ceremonial painting

programme.desc: Tom goes to school to explain himself to the headmaster. Viv follows Donna to find out where Kelly is staying. Rachel spreads the gossip about Eric getting engaged

programme.title: Heartbeat

Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's birthday celebrations give her the blues, and even Wilfred's arrival doesn't make her any happier

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is interviewed by the police about the hijacking of one of his lorries five years ago. Marlon attempts a set-up to make it look like the Dingle pigs have been stolen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie hands Frank her resignation, and he is desperate to find Kim before she can do anything else to ruin him. Vic and Viv have a public row in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is sickened to find she and Steve are on the front page of the Gazette, and the McDonalds visit Steve in prison. Tricia starts work at Baldwin's Sportswear

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Betty tells Alec that Vicky needs a fresh start somewhere else. Vera and Jack spy on each other to catch the other dipping into the till. Anne's parents do not want to meet Andy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: With their friendship in tatters, the lack of communication between Rachel and Janet is making even the simplest of tasks difficult, and it takes a call about Gill's possible abduction to reunite them as a team. The duo soon have Gill's car in their sights and identify the kidnapper, but discover they are heading for a notorious suicide location. Can the detectives get to their boss in time?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby lets a room to a young couple he believes have just got married - unaware that they are not man and wife. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned confronts Chris with a loaded gun when he hears he attempted to rape Linda. She tells Biff that her dad knows about Chris and they head off to find them

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony is glad Steve is in prison as he was always bothering Fiona. Andy decides to see Anne's parents, and Audrey thinks Alma wants to get her claws into Stephen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis hates having Norris to stay, and Raquel tries to drum up trade but no one wants a massage. Steve is thrilled when Fiona visits him and tells her he is sorry for everything

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A vulnerable young man is reported missing by his pub landlord boss. Syndicate 9 suspect Robin McKendrick may have been killed after a photo of him bound and gagged in a car boot appears online. However, the case takes an unusual turn when a body found in a flooded quarry turns out to be a woman's corpse. Meanwhile, battle-scarred from their conflict last year, Scott and Bailey are determined to move forward with honesty. The pair find themselves in front of the promotion board and pass with flying colours, leaving Gill with the task of choosing who stays on as sergeant at the department. Danny Webb joins a cast led by Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is furious with her father for attacking Chris, and Frank tells the Glovers that he wants to make amends for Chris's treatment of Linda. The police look for a missing Kim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police speak to Frank about Kim's disappearance. Tom is given the job in Aberystwyth but tells Kelly she cannot come with him. Eric warns Rachel to keep out of his affairs

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek convinces himself that Norris has murdered Angela. Liz and Jim walk in on Vicky's farewell party, and Betty gives her the hot pot recipe as a leaving present

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts are alarmed when their door opens and Terry Duckworth walks in with Tommy. Vera is thrilled to see her grandson, but apprehensive about Terry's return

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Priya confronts Sarah, Harriet is stopped in her tracks, and Dan lashes out at Amelia

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Tracy receive surprising news, Priya decides to confront Al, and Gabby is feeling fed-up

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy and Nate have a difficult conversation, Nicola feels for Gabby, and Sam lets slip private information about Lydia

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fox tells the court how Vicky bribed him to change his statement, and her barrister paints a picture of Steve taking advantage and using her money

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is distraught over Steve's sentence and the fact Vicky walked free. The Duckworths return from Spain and Betty is relieved to hand the pub back to them

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tom goes to school to explain himself to the headmaster. Viv follows Donna to find out where Kelly is staying. Rachel spreads the gossip about Eric getting engaged

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers visit Dave's grave on what would have been his birthday. Frank and Chris are arrested for conspiracy to commit fraud. Eric's fiancee Dee arrives in the village

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's birthday celebrations give her the blues, and even Wilfred's arrival doesn't make her any happier

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is interviewed by the police about the hijacking of one of his lorries five years ago. Marlon attempts a set-up to make it look like the Dingle pigs have been stolen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie hands Frank her resignation, and he is desperate to find Kim before she can do anything else to ruin him. Vic and Viv have a public row in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is sickened to find she and Steve are on the front page of the Gazette, and the McDonalds visit Steve in prison. Tricia starts work at Baldwin's Sportswear

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Betty tells Alec that Vicky needs a fresh start somewhere else. Vera and Jack spy on each other to catch the other dipping into the till. Anne's parents do not want to meet Andy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: With their friendship in tatters, the lack of communication between Rachel and Janet is making even the simplest of tasks difficult, and it takes a call about Gill's possible abduction to reunite them as a team. The duo soon have Gill's car in their sights and identify the kidnapper, but discover they are heading for a notorious suicide location. Can the detectives get to their boss in time?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is furious with her father for attacking Chris, and Frank tells the Glovers that he wants to make amends for Chris's treatment of Linda. The police look for a missing Kim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police speak to Frank about Kim's disappearance. Tom is given the job in Aberystwyth but tells Kelly she cannot come with him. Eric warns Rachel to keep out of his affairs

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek convinces himself that Norris has murdered Angela. Liz and Jim walk in on Vicky's farewell party, and Betty gives her the hot pot recipe as a leaving present

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts are alarmed when their door opens and Terry Duckworth walks in with Tommy. Vera is thrilled to see her grandson, but apprehensive about Terry's return

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: The comedian performs a variety of sketches, with the assistance of guest Vincent Price

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a people smuggling ring, focusing his inquiries on the activities of a local yacht club. When it is revealed that one of the immigrants has gone on the run while suffering from smallpox, the Saint sets out to locate him before he can spread the disease

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The outlaws enter a mysterious village where their every wish is granted - unaware they are being duped by the evil Gulnar, who is intent on reviving a bloodthirsty pagan deity. Jason Connery stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey comes up against a man who is his equal in all respects. The only difference is that he's on the wrong side of the law. Obsessed with capturing him, Dempsey tests Makepeace's patience to the limit. Will Dempsey succeed against impossible odds?

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur excludes Terry from his latest business venture, prompting him to embark on a search for alternative employment. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A suspect collapses while being questioned and rushed to hospital with injuries likely to have been incurred in an assault. Though innocent, Regan finds himself targeted by the man's vengeful brother who thinks the detective is responsible for his sibling's condition. Gwen Taylor guest stars in the police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Exasperated at the Sheriff's record in office, King John visits Sherwood to personally oversee the destruction of Robin and his outlaw band - a task he entrusts to the ruthless Roger de Carnac. Phil Davis and Matt Frewer guest star

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Part one. Dempsey goes undercover as a small-time crook to snare an underhanded police commissioner

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: An ex-footballer persuades Arthur to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match. However, trouble ensues once it becomes apparent they are forgeries. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a pub quiz team from Oxfordshire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a volcanic island in the Pacific Ring of Fire, an ancient fishing community is being terrorised by something in the water that has left behind a trail of bloodied victims. With a collective knowledge built up over centuries, these fishermen thought they knew everything in these waters - until now. The only clue they have to the attacker's identity are the large puncture wounds it has left behind. Here, angling detective Jeremy Wade journeys to this land of fire and folklore to put himself to the ultimate test - can he uncover the identity of this mysterious fanged assailant?

programme.title: Assassins (1995)
programme.desc: Hitman Robert Rath is planning to quit his lethal profession, especially when he is stalked by a ruthless younger assassin who has no qualms about killing innocent bystanders. But as Rath takes on his last assignment, he decides to protect his intended target after his rival comes gunning for her. Thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas and Julianne Moore

programme.title: Police Academy (1984)
programme.desc: An American police college lowers its entrance requirements in the hope of attracting new recruits, and in response, assorted dubious candidates turn up for enrolment. A short-tempered instructor is determined to weed out the bad apples, but a riot provides the clueless cops with a chance to prove their worth. Comedy, starring Steve Guttenberg, Kim Cattrall, Bubba Smith and GW Bailey

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Carter's wife Alison is targeted by a hit-and-run driver while performing a routine errand on his behalf. programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: The thief encounters a deposed king-turned-crooked gambler

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The sheriff captures Much the miller's son and threatens to hang him, and an ageing outlaw kidnap's the tyrant's nephew. Drama, starring Jason Coney

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey is accused of murdering a small-time criminal, forcing Makepeace to put her career on the line as she attempts to uncover evidence to support his claims of innocence. Detective drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A wealthy family insists Dr Sudbury, a hypnotist friend of Arthur's, has mesmerised its teenage daughter Rita into agreeing to marry him. Arthur finds himself immersed in the fracas, while simultaneously trying to take over the affairs of a steam-cleaning business. Light-hearted drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of family and friends from Middlesbrough answers questions from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove head to an automotive show in Chippenham, where they look at vintage oil cans, enamel signs and classic bikes and cars

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates the documentary featuring another selection of real-life incidents captured by people on their personal cameras. Footage includes the woman who had a close shave when a car ploughed into her office, parallel parking calamities and the new driver with no beginners' luck

programme.title: Passenger 57 (1992)
programme.desc: After his wife is shot dead during a robbery, a cop hands in his badge and takes up a job offer from an old friend to be a security adviser for a major transatlantic airline. On the flight taking him to his new employers in Los Angeles is an international terrorist being escorted to trial by the FBI - while the prisoner's accomplices are disguised as passengers and crew and plan to hijack the plane. Action thriller, with Wesley Snipes, Bruce Payne, Tom Sizemore, Alex Datcher, Bruce Greenwood and Elizabeth Hurley
